    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 615
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/615](https://www.erdosproblems.com/615)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDoes there exist some constant $c > 0$ such that if $G$ is a graph with $n$ vertices
    25‚Üíand $\geq (1/8 - c)n^2$ edges then $G$ must contain either a $K_4$ or an independent set
    26‚Üíon at least $n / \log n$ vertices?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíEquivalently, if $\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$ denotes the Ramsey-Tur√°n number (maximum number of
    29‚Üíedges in a $K_k$-free graph on $n$ vertices with independence number $< \ell$), the
    30‚Üíquestion asks whether $\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, \lceil n / \log n \rceil) < (1/8 - c)n^2$ for some
    31‚Üí$c > 0$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Hajnal, Simonovits, S√≥s, and Szemer√©di [EHSSS93].
    34‚Üí
    35‚ÜíErd≈ës, Hajnal, S√≥s, and Szemer√©di [EHSS83] proved that for any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$,
    36‚Üí$$\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, \varepsilon n) < (1/8 + o(1))n^2.$$
    37‚Üí
    38‚ÜíSudakov [Su03] proved that $\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, n \cdot e^{-f(n)}) = o(n^2)$ whenever
    39‚Üí$f(n)/\sqrt{\log n} \to \infty$.
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíDisproved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15], who showed that
    42‚Üí$\mathrm{rt}(n; 4, n \cdot e^{-f(n)}) \geq (1/8 - o(1))n^2$
    43‚Üíwhenever $f(n) = o(\sqrt{\log n / \log \log n})$.
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí**References:**
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[EHSSS93] Erd≈ës, P., Hajnal, A., Simonovits, M., S√≥s, V. T., and Szemer√©di, E. (1993).
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[EHSS83] Erd≈ës, P., Hajnal, A., S√≥s, V. T., and Szemer√©di, E. (1983).
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí[Su03] Sudakov, B. (2003).
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí[FLZ15] Fox, J., Loh, P.-S., and Zhao, Y. (2015).
    54‚Üí-/
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Classical
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üínamespace Erdos615
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- A set of vertices is independent in $G$ if no two distinct vertices in the set
    61‚Üí    are adjacent. -/
    62‚Üídef IsIndependentSet {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
    63‚Üí  ‚àÄ u ‚àà S, ‚àÄ v ‚àà S, u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- The Ramsey-Tur√°n number $\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$: the maximum number of edges in a graph
    66‚Üí    on $n$ vertices that contains no $k$-clique and has no independent set of size
    67‚Üí    $\geq \ell$. Returns $0$ if no such graph exists. -/
    68‚Üínoncomputable def ramseyTuranNumber (n k ‚Ñì : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    69‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    70‚Üí    G.edgeFinset.card = m ‚àß G.CliqueFree k ‚àß
    71‚Üí    ‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚Üí S.card < ‚Ñì}
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/--
    74‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 615** (DISPROVED) [EHSSS93]:
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíDoes there exist $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$, every graph $G$
    77‚Üíon $n$ vertices with at least $(1/8 - c)n^2$ edges contains either a $K_4$ or an
    78‚Üíindependent set of size at least $n / \log n$?
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíDisproved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15].
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    83‚Üítheorem erdos_615 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    84‚Üí    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    85‚Üí      ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    86‚Üí        (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (1 / 8 - c) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 ‚Üí
    87‚Üí        (¬¨G.CliqueFree 4) ‚à®
    88‚Üí        ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• (n : ‚Ñù) / Real.log (n : ‚Ñù) ‚àß
    89‚Üí          IsIndependentSet G S := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/--
    93‚Üí**Erd≈ës-Hajnal-S√≥s-Szemer√©di** [EHSS83]:
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíFor any fixed $\varepsilon > 0$, every $K_4$-free graph on $n$ vertices with independence
    96‚Üínumber $< \varepsilon n$ has fewer than $(1/8 + o(1))n^2$ edges.
    97‚Üí-/
    98‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    99‚Üítheorem erdos_615.variants.EHSS (Œµ : ‚Ñù) (hŒµ : Œµ > 0) :
   100‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œ¥ : ‚Ñù, Œ¥ > 0 ‚Üí ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
   101‚Üí      ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
   102‚Üí        G.CliqueFree 4 ‚Üí
   103‚Üí        (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚Üí (S.card : ‚Ñù) < Œµ * (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
   104‚Üí        (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ (1 / 8 + Œ¥) * (n : ‚Ñù) ^ 2 := by
   105‚Üí  sorry
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üíend Erdos615

    16‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 501
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/501](https://www.erdosproblems.com/501)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíFor every $x \in \mathbb{R}$, let $A_x \subset \mathbb{R}$ be a bounded set with outer measure
    25‚Üí$< 1$. Must there exist an infinite independent set, that is, some infinite $X \subseteq \mathbb{R}$
    26‚Üísuch that $x \notin A_y$ for all $x \neq y \in X$?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíIf the sets $A_x$ are closed and have measure $< 1$, then must there exist an
    29‚Üíindependent set of size $3$?
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíErd≈ës and Hajnal proved the existence of arbitrarily large finite independent
    32‚Üísets (under the assumptions in the first problem). Hechler showed the answer
    33‚Üíto the first question is no, assuming the continuum hypothesis. Newelski,
    34‚ÜíPawlikowski, and Seredy≈Ñski proved that if all the $A_x$ are closed with
    35‚Üímeasure $< 1$ then there is an infinite independent set.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[Er61] Erd≈ës, P., _Problems in combinatorics_ (1961).
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[ErHa71] Erd≈ës, P. and Hajnal, A., _On a property of families of sets_ (1971).
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üíopen MeasureTheory Set
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üínamespace Erdos501
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- A set $X$ is independent with respect to a family of sets $A$ if for all
    47‚Üídistinct $x, y \in X$, we have $x \notin A(y)$. -/
    48‚Üídef IndependentFamily (A : ‚Ñù ‚Üí Set ‚Ñù) (X : Set ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
    49‚Üí  ‚àÄ x ‚àà X, ‚àÄ y ‚àà X, x ‚âÝ y ‚Üí x ‚àâ A y
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/--
    52‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 501 [Er61][ErHa71]: If each $A_x$ is bounded with outer measure $< 1$, must
    53‚Üíthere exist an infinite independent set?
    54‚Üí
    55‚ÜíHechler showed the answer is no assuming the continuum hypothesis. The full
    56‚ÜíZFC status remains open.
    57‚Üí-/
    58‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 3 28]
    59‚Üítheorem erdos_501 :
    60‚Üí    answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    61‚Üí    ‚àÄ A : ‚Ñù ‚Üí Set ‚Ñù,
    62‚Üí      (‚àÄ x : ‚Ñù, Bornology.IsBounded (A x) ‚àß volume (A x) < 1) ‚Üí
    63‚Üí      ‚àÉ X : Set ‚Ñù, X.Infinite ‚àß IndependentFamily A X := by
    64‚Üí  sorry
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí/--
    67‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 501, closed variant [Er61][ErHa71]: If each $A_x$ is closed with
    68‚Üímeasure $< 1$, must there exist an independent set of size $3$?
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíNewelski, Pawlikowski, and Seredy≈Ñski proved that under these hypotheses there
    71‚Üíis in fact an infinite independent set.
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 3 28]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_501.variants.closed_measure :
    75‚Üí    answer(True) ‚Üî
    76‚Üí    ‚àÄ A : ‚Ñù ‚Üí Set ‚Ñù,
    77‚Üí      (‚àÄ x : ‚Ñù, IsClosed (A x) ‚àß volume (A x) < 1) ‚Üí
    78‚Üí      ‚àÉ x y z : ‚Ñù, x ‚âÝ y ‚àß y ‚âÝ z ‚àß x ‚âÝ z ‚àß
    79‚Üí        IndependentFamily A {x, y, z} := by
    80‚Üí  sorry
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üíend Erdos501

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 151
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíErd≈ës and Gallai conjectured that the clique transversal number of any graph on $n$ vertices
    23‚Üíis at most $n - H(n)$, where $H(n)$ is the minimum independence number over all triangle-free
    24‚Üígraphs on $n$ vertices.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/151](https://www.erdosproblems.com/151)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[Er88] Erd≈ës, P., _Problems and results on chromatic numbers in finite and infinite graphs_,
    29‚Üí1988, p.82.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[EGT92] Erd≈ës, P., Gallai, T. and Tuza, Zs., _Covering the cliques of a graph with vertices_,
    32‚Üí1992, p.280.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos151
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- $S$ is a maximal clique of $G$ (represented as a `Finset`): it is a clique and
    40‚Üíno vertex outside $S$ can be added while preserving the clique property. -/
    41‚Üídef IsMaximalCliqueFS {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  G.IsClique (S : Set (Fin n)) ‚àß
    43‚Üí  ‚àÄ v : Fin n, v ‚àâ S ‚Üí ¬¨G.IsClique (‚Üë(insert v S) : Set (Fin n))
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- $T$ is a clique transversal of $G$ if $T$ has non-empty intersection with every
    46‚Üímaximal clique of $G$ that has at least $2$ vertices. -/
    47‚Üídef IsCliqueTransversal {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (T : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    48‚Üí  ‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsMaximalCliqueFS G S ‚Üí 2 ‚â§ S.card ‚Üí (T ‚à© S).Nonempty
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The clique transversal number $\tau(G)$: the minimum cardinality of a clique
    51‚Üítransversal of $G$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def cliqueTransversalNumber {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ‚Ñï :=
    53‚Üí  sInf { k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ T : Finset (Fin n), IsCliqueTransversal G T ‚àß T.card = k }
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- $S$ is an independent set in $G$: no two distinct vertices of $S$ are adjacent. -/
    56‚Üídef IsIndependentSet {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    57‚Üí  ‚àÄ u v : Fin n, u ‚àà S ‚Üí v ‚àà S ‚Üí u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/-- The independence number $\alpha(G)$: the maximum cardinality of an independent set. -/
    60‚Üínoncomputable def independenceNumber {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ‚Ñï :=
    61‚Üí  sSup { k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentSet G S ‚àß S.card = k }
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- $H(n)$ is maximal such that every triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices contains
    64‚Üían independent set of size $H(n)$; equivalently, $H(n)$ is the minimum
    65‚Üíindependence number over all triangle-free graphs on $n$ vertices. -/
    66‚Üínoncomputable def H (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    67‚Üí  sInf { k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), G.CliqueFree 3 ‚àß independenceNumber G = k }
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/--
    70‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 151 [Er88, p.82] [EGT92, p.280] (problem of Erd≈ës and Gallai):
    71‚ÜíIf $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices then $\tau(G) \leq n - H(n)$,
    72‚Üíwhere $\tau(G)$ is the clique transversal number of $G$ and $H(n)$ is the maximum $k$
    73‚Üísuch that every triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices contains an independent set
    74‚Üíof size $k$.
    75‚Üí-/
    76‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    77‚Üítheorem erdos_151 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    78‚Üí    ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    79‚Üí      cliqueTransversalNumber G ‚â§ n - H n := by
    80‚Üí  sorry
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üíend Erdos151
    83‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 993
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/993](https://www.erdosproblems.com/993)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA question of Alavi, Malde, Schwenk, and Erd≈ës [AMSE87], who showed that the independent set
    25‚Üísequence is not constrained for general graphs (in fact every possible pattern of inequalities
    26‚Üíis achieved by some graph).
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[AMSE87] Alavi, Y., Malde, P. J., Schwenk, A. J., and Erd≈ës, P., *The vertex independence
    29‚Üísequence of a graph is not constrained*. Congressus Numerantium (1987), 58, 15-23.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos993
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- The number of independent sets of size $k$ in a simple graph $G$.
    37‚ÜíAn independent set is a set of vertices that are pairwise non-adjacent. -/
    38‚Üídef numIndepSets {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    39‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (k : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    40‚Üí  (Finset.univ.powerset.filter (fun s : Finset V =>
    41‚Üí    s.card = k ‚àß ‚àÄ ‚¶Éu‚¶Ñ, u ‚àà s ‚Üí ‚àÄ ‚¶Év‚¶Ñ, v ‚àà s ‚Üí u ‚âÝ v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)).card
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 993 [AMSE87]:
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíThe independent set sequence of any tree or forest is unimodal. That is, if
    47‚Üí$i_k(T)$ counts the number of independent sets of vertices of size $k$ in a
    48‚Üíforest $T$, then there exists $m \geq 0$ such that
    49‚Üí$$
    50‚Üíi_0(T) \leq i_1(T) \leq \cdots \leq i_m(T) \geq i_{m+1}(T) \geq i_{m+2}(T) \geq \cdots
    51‚Üí$$
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_993 {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    55‚Üí    (T : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel T.Adj] (hT : T.IsAcyclic) :
    56‚Üí    ‚àÉ m : ‚Ñï, (‚àÄ i j : ‚Ñï, i ‚â§ j ‚Üí j ‚â§ m ‚Üí numIndepSets T i ‚â§ numIndepSets T j) ‚àß
    57‚Üí             (‚àÄ i j : ‚Ñï, m ‚â§ i ‚Üí i ‚â§ j ‚Üí numIndepSets T j ‚â§ numIndepSets T i) := by
    58‚Üí  sorry
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üíend Erdos993

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 928
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/928](https://www.erdosproblems.com/928)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $\alpha, \beta \in (0,1)$ and let $P(n)$ denote the largest prime divisor of $n$.
    25‚ÜíDoes the density of integers $n$ such that $P(n) < n^\alpha$ and $P(n+1) < (n+1)^\beta$
    26‚Üíexist?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíErd≈ës asked whether the events $P(n) < n^\alpha$ and $P(n+1) < (n+1)^\beta$ are independent,
    29‚Üíin the sense that the density of $n$ satisfying both conditions equals
    30‚Üí$\rho(1/\alpha) \cdot \rho(1/\beta)$, where $\rho$ is the Dickman function.
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíDickman [Di30] showed the density of smooth $n$, with largest prime factor $< n^\alpha$,
    33‚Üíis $\rho(1/\alpha)$.
    34‚Üí
    35‚ÜíTer√§v√§inen [Te18] proved the logarithmic density exists and equals
    36‚Üí$\rho(1/\alpha) \rho(1/\beta)$.
    37‚ÜíWang [Wa21] proved the natural density equals $\rho(1/\alpha) \rho(1/\beta)$ assuming the
    38‚ÜíElliott‚ÄìHalberstam conjecture for friable integers.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[Er76d] Erd≈ës, P., *Problems in number theory and combinatorics*.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[ErPo78] Erd≈ës, P. and Pomerance, C.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[Di30] Dickman, K., *On the frequency of numbers containing prime factors of a certain
    45‚Üírelative magnitude*. Ark. Mat. Astr. Fys. **22A** (1930), 1‚Äì14.
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[Te18] Ter√§v√§inen, J., *On binary correlations of multiplicative functions*. Forum Math.
    48‚ÜíSigma **6** (2018), e10.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí[Wa21] Wang, Z., *On the density of integers with prescribed largest prime factor and
    51‚Üísmooth neighbours*. (2021).
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üíopen Set Filter
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üínamespace Erdos928
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- The largest prime factor of $n$, or $0$ if $n \le 1$. -/
    59‚Üínoncomputable def largestPrimeFactor (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    60‚Üí  if h : n.primeFactors.Nonempty then n.primeFactors.max' h else 0
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- The Dickman function $\rho : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$, the unique continuous function
    63‚Üísatisfying $\rho(u) = 1$ for $0 \le u \le 1$ and $u \rho'(u) = -\rho(u-1)$ for $u > 1$. -/
    64‚Üíopaque dickmanRho : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí/-- The natural density of a set $S \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ exists and equals $d$. -/
    67‚Üídef HasNaturalDensity (S : Set ‚Ñï) (d : ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
    68‚Üí  Filter.Tendsto (fun N : ‚Ñï => ((S ‚à© {i | 1 ‚â§ i ‚àß i ‚â§ N}).ncard : ‚Ñù) / N)
    69‚Üí    atTop (nhds d)
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- The set of $n \ge 2$ with $P(n) < n^\alpha$ and $P(n+1) < (n+1)^\beta$. -/
    72‚Üídef smoothConsecutiveSet (Œ± Œ≤ : ‚Ñù) : Set ‚Ñï :=
    73‚Üí  {n : ‚Ñï | n ‚â• 2 ‚àß
    74‚Üí    (largestPrimeFactor n : ‚Ñù) < (n : ‚Ñù) ^ Œ± ‚àß
    75‚Üí    (largestPrimeFactor (n + 1) : ‚Ñù) < ((n + 1 : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñù) ^ Œ≤}
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí/--
    78‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 928 [Er76d][ErPo78]:
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíFor $\alpha, \beta \in (0,1)$, the natural density of integers $n$ with $P(n) < n^\alpha$ and
    81‚Üí$P(n+1) < (n+1)^\beta$ exists and equals $\rho(1/\alpha) \cdot \rho(1/\beta)$, where $\rho$ is
    82‚Üíthe Dickman function. This asserts independence of the smooth-number events for consecutive
    83‚Üíintegers.
    84‚Üí-/
    85‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    86‚Üítheorem erdos_928 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    87‚Üí    ‚àÄ Œ± Œ≤ : ‚Ñù, 0 < Œ± ‚Üí Œ± < 1 ‚Üí 0 < Œ≤ ‚Üí Œ≤ < 1 ‚Üí
    88‚Üí    HasNaturalDensity (smoothConsecutiveSet Œ± Œ≤)
    89‚Üí      (dickmanRho (1 / Œ±) * dickmanRho (1 / Œ≤)) := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üíend Erdos928

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 804
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/804](https://www.erdosproblems.com/804)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(m,n)$ be maximal such that any graph on $n$ vertices in which every induced
    25‚Üísubgraph on $m$ vertices has an independent set of size at least $\log n$ must
    26‚Üícontain an independent set of size at least $f(m,n)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíEstimate $f(n)$. In particular, is it true that $f((\log n)^2, n) \geq n^{1/2 - o(1)}$?
    29‚ÜíIs it true that $f((\log n)^3, n) \gg (\log n)^3$?
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíA question of Erd≈ës and Hajnal [Er91]. DISPROVED by Alon and Sudakov [AlSu07],
    32‚Üíwho proved that
    33‚Üí$$(\log n)^2 / \log(\log n) \ll f((\log n)^2, n) \ll (\log n)^2$$
    34‚Üíand
    35‚Üí$$f((\log n)^3, n) \asymp (\log n)^2 / \log(\log n).$$
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen Classical SimpleGraph Finset
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos804
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- An independent set in a simple graph: a finset of vertices with no edges
    43‚Üí    between any two of its members. -/
    44‚Üídef IsIndepSet {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  ‚àÄ u ‚àà S, ‚àÄ v ‚àà S, ¬¨G.Adj u v
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- The independence number of a graph on $n$ vertices: the maximum cardinality
    48‚Üí    of an independent set. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def independenceNum {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ‚Ñï :=
    50‚Üí  ((univ : Finset (Fin n)).powerset.filter (IsIndepSet G)).sup Finset.card
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- $f(m, n)$ is the largest $k$ such that every graph on $n$ vertices
    53‚Üí    in which every induced subgraph on $m$ vertices has an independent set of
    54‚Üí    size $\geq \lceil \log n \rceil$ must have independence number $\geq k$.
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí    Equivalently, the infimum of independence numbers over all qualifying
    57‚Üí    graphs. -/
    58‚Üínoncomputable def erdos804_f (m n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    59‚Üí  sInf { k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    60‚Üí    (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), S.card = m ‚Üí
    61‚Üí      ‚àÉ T : Finset (Fin n), T ‚äÜ S ‚àß T.card ‚â• Nat.ceil (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚àß
    62‚Üí        IsIndepSet G T) ‚àß
    63‚Üí    independenceNum G = k }
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/--
    66‚ÜíAlon-Sudakov [AlSu07] upper bound: $f((\log n)^2, n) \leq C \cdot (\log n)^2$
    67‚Üífor some constant $C > 0$ and all sufficiently large $n$.
    68‚Üí
    69‚ÜíThis disproves the conjecture of Erd≈ës and Hajnal that
    70‚Üí$f((\log n)^2, n) \geq n^{1/2 - o(1)}$.
    71‚Üí-/
    72‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    73‚Üítheorem erdos_804 :
    74‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    75‚Üí      (erdos804_f (Nat.floor ((Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 2)) n : ‚Ñù) ‚â§
    76‚Üí        C * (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 2 := by
    77‚Üí  sorry
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/--
    80‚ÜíAlon-Sudakov [AlSu07] lower bound: $f((\log n)^2, n) \geq c \cdot (\log n)^2 / \log(\log n)$
    81‚Üífor some constant $c > 0$ and all sufficiently large $n$.
    82‚Üí-/
    83‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    84‚Üítheorem erdos_804.variants.part1_lower :
    85‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    86‚Üí      (erdos804_f (Nat.floor ((Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 2)) n : ‚Ñù) ‚â•
    87‚Üí        C * (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 2 / Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) := by
    88‚Üí  sorry
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí/--
    91‚ÜíAlon-Sudakov [AlSu07] upper bound: $f((\log n)^3, n) \leq C \cdot (\log n)^2 / \log(\log n)$
    92‚Üífor some constant $C > 0$ and all sufficiently large $n$.
    93‚Üí

    96‚Üí-/
    97‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    98‚Üítheorem erdos_804.variants.part2_upper :
    99‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
   100‚Üí      (erdos804_f (Nat.floor ((Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 3)) n : ‚Ñù) ‚â§
   101‚Üí        C * (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 2 / Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) := by
   102‚Üí  sorry
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí/--
   105‚ÜíAlon-Sudakov [AlSu07] lower bound: $f((\log n)^3, n) \geq c \cdot (\log n)^2 / \log(\log n)$
   106‚Üífor some constant $c > 0$ and all sufficiently large $n$.
   107‚Üí-/
   108‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
   109‚Üítheorem erdos_804.variants.part2_lower :
   110‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
   111‚Üí      (erdos804_f (Nat.floor ((Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 3)) n : ‚Ñù) ‚â•
   112‚Üí        C * (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) ^ 2 / Real.log (Real.log (n : ‚Ñù)) := by

    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 807
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/807](https://www.erdosproblems.com/807)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIs it true that for a random graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with edge probability $1/2$,
    25‚Üí$\tau(G) = n - \alpha(G)$ almost surely? Here $\tau(G)$ is the bipartition number (the
    26‚Üísmallest number of pairwise edge-disjoint complete bipartite graphs whose union is $G$)
    27‚Üíand $\alpha(G)$ is the independence number. Disproved by Alon [Al15].
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Al15] Alon, N., _Bipartite decomposition of random graphs_, J. Graph Theory, 2015.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[ABH17] Alon, N., Bohman, T. and Huang, H., _More on bipartite decomposition of random
    32‚Üígraphs_, J. Graph Theory, 2017.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Filter Classical
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üíopen scoped Topology
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üínamespace Erdos807
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíA biclique cover of a simple graph $G$ of size $k$ consists of $k$ complete bipartite
    43‚Üísubgraphs (specified by pairs of disjoint vertex sets) that are pairwise
    44‚Üíedge-disjoint and together cover all edges of $G$.
    45‚Üí-/
    46‚Üídef IsBicliqueCover {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ñï)
    47‚Üí    (A B : Fin k ‚Üí Set V) : Prop :=
    48‚Üí  (‚àÄ i, Disjoint (A i) (B i)) ‚àß
    49‚Üí  (‚àÄ i, ‚àÄ a ‚àà A i, ‚àÄ b ‚àà B i, G.Adj a b) ‚àß
    50‚Üí  (‚àÄ i j : Fin k, i ‚âÝ j ‚Üí
    51‚Üí    ‚àÄ v w : V, ¬¨((v ‚àà A i ‚àß w ‚àà B i ‚à® w ‚àà A i ‚àß v ‚àà B i) ‚àß
    52‚Üí                  (v ‚àà A j ‚àß w ‚àà B j ‚à® w ‚àà A j ‚àß v ‚àà B j))) ‚àß
    53‚Üí  (‚àÄ v w : V, G.Adj v w ‚Üí
    54‚Üí    ‚àÉ i : Fin k, (v ‚àà A i ‚àß w ‚àà B i) ‚à® (w ‚àà A i ‚àß v ‚àà B i))
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíThe bipartition number $\tau(G)$ is the minimum number of pairwise edge-disjoint
    58‚Üícomplete bipartite subgraphs needed to cover all edges of $G$.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üínoncomputable def bipartitionNumber {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï :=
    61‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ (A B : Fin k ‚Üí Set V), IsBicliqueCover G k A B}
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/--
    64‚ÜíThe independence number $\alpha(G)$ of a finite graph is the maximum cardinality of
    65‚Üían independent set (a set of vertices with no edges between them).
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def independenceNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V] (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï :=
    68‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ S : Finset V, S.card = k ‚àß
    69‚Üí    ‚àÄ v ‚àà S, ‚àÄ w ‚àà S, v ‚âÝ w ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj v w}
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/--
    72‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 807 (disproved by Alon [Al15]):
    73‚Üí
    74‚ÜíIs it true that for a random graph $G$ on $n$ vertices with edge probability $1/2$,
    75‚Üí$\tau(G) = n - \alpha(G)$ almost surely?
    76‚Üí
    77‚ÜíThe bipartition number $\tau(G)$ is the smallest number of pairwise edge-disjoint
    78‚Üícomplete bipartite graphs whose union is $G$. The independence number $\alpha(G)$ is
    79‚Üíthe size of the largest independent set in $G$.
    80‚Üí
    81‚ÜíIn the $G(n, 1/2)$ model every labeled graph on $n$ vertices is equally likely, so
    82‚Üí"almost surely" means the proportion of graphs satisfying the property tends
    83‚Üíto $1$ as $n \to \infty$.
    84‚Üí
    85‚ÜíAlon [Al15] showed this is false: almost surely $\tau(G) \leq n - \alpha(G) - 1$.
    86‚ÜíAlon, Bohman, and Huang [ABH17] proved there exists $c > 0$ such that
    87‚Üíalmost surely $\tau(G) \leq n - (1 + c)\alpha(G)$.
    88‚Üí-/
    89‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 60]
    90‚Üítheorem erdos_807 : answer(False) ‚Üî
    91‚Üí    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï =>
    92‚Üí      (Nat.card {G : SimpleGraph (Fin n) //
    93‚Üí        bipartitionNumber G = n - independenceNumber G} : ‚Ñù) /
    94‚Üí      (Nat.card (SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ‚Ñù))
    95‚Üí    atTop (nhds 1) := by
    96‚Üí  sorry
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üíend Erdos807
    99‚Üí

    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 636
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/636](https://www.erdosproblems.com/636)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSuppose $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices which contains no complete graph or independent
    25‚Üíset on $\gg \log n$ many vertices. Must $G$ contain $\gg n^{5/2}$ induced subgraphs which
    26‚Üípairwise differ in either the number of vertices or the number of edges?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Faudree, and S√≥s [Er93, p.346], who proved there exist
    29‚Üí$\gg n^{3/2}$ many such subgraphs, and note that $n^{5/2}$ would be best possible.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíThis was proved by Kwan and Sudakov [KwSu21].
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Er93] Erd≈ës, P., _On some of my favourite theorems_ (1993), p.346.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Er97d] Erd≈ës, P., _Some of my new and almost new problems and results in
    36‚Üícombinatorics and graph theory_ (1997).
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[KwSu21] Kwan, M. and Sudakov, B., _Proof of a conjecture on induced subgraphs
    39‚Üíof Ramsey graphs_ (2021).
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üínamespace Erdos636
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- The set of distinct (vertex count, edge count) signatures realized by induced
    47‚Üísubgraphs of a graph $G$ on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$. For each subset $S$ of vertices,
    48‚Üíwe record $(|S|, |E(G[S])|)$ where $G[S]$ is the induced subgraph on $S$. -/
    49‚Üínoncomputable def inducedSubgraphSignatures {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) :
    50‚Üí    Finset (‚Ñï √ó ‚Ñï) :=
    51‚Üí  Finset.univ.image fun S : Finset (Fin n) =>
    52‚Üí    (S.card, (G.induce (‚ÜëS : Set (Fin n))).edgeSet.ncard)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 636** [Er93, p.346][Er97d]:
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíFor every $C > 0$, there exist $c > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$, if $G$ is a
    58‚Üígraph on $n$ vertices with clique number at most $C \cdot \log_2(n)$ and independence number
    59‚Üíat most $C \cdot \log_2(n)$, then $G$ has at least $c \cdot n^{5/2}$ distinct induced subgraph
    60‚Üísignatures (pairs (vertex count, edge count) among all induced subgraphs of $G$).
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíProved by Kwan and Sudakov [KwSu21].
    63‚Üí-/
    64‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    65‚Üítheorem erdos_636 :
    66‚Üí    answer(True) ‚Üî
    67‚Üí    ‚àÄ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚Üí
    68‚Üí    ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß
    69‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    70‚Üí    ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    71‚Üí    (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), G.IsClique (‚ÜëS : Set (Fin n)) ‚Üí
    72‚Üí      (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * Real.logb 2 (‚Üën : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
    73‚Üí    (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), G·∂ú.IsClique (‚ÜëS : Set (Fin n)) ‚Üí
    74‚Üí      (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * Real.logb 2 (‚Üën : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
    75‚Üí    ((inducedSubgraphSignatures G).card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• c * (‚Üën : ‚Ñù) ^ ((5 : ‚Ñù) / 2) := by
    76‚Üí  sorry
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üíend Erdos636
    79‚Üí

    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 637
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/637](https://www.erdosproblems.com/637)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices which contains no complete graph or independent
    25‚Üíset on $\gg \log n$ vertices then $G$ contains an induced subgraph on $\gg n$ vertices
    26‚Üíwhich contains $\gg n^{1/2}$ distinct degrees.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Faudree, and S√≥s [Er97d].
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThis was proved by Bukh and Sudakov [BuSu07].
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíJenssen, Keevash, Long, and Yepremyan [JKLY20] proved that there must exist
    33‚Üían induced subgraph which contains $\gg n^{2/3}$ distinct degrees (with no
    34‚Üírestriction on the number of vertices).
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[Er97d] Erd≈ës, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of
    37‚Üícombinatorics_, 1997.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[BuSu07] Bukh, B. and Sudakov, B., _Induced subgraphs of Ramsey graphs with
    40‚Üímany distinct degrees_, 2007.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[JKLY20] Jenssen, M., Keevash, P., Long, E. and Yepremyan, L., _Distinct
    43‚Üídegrees in induced subgraphs_, 2020.
    44‚Üí-/
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Classical
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üínamespace Erdos637
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The degree of vertex $v$ in the induced subgraph $G[S]$. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def inducedDegree {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    52‚Üí    (S : Finset (Fin n)) (v : Fin n) : ‚Ñï :=
    53‚Üí  (S.filter fun w => G.Adj v w).card
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- The number of distinct degrees realized by vertices of $S$ in the induced
    56‚Üí    subgraph $G[S]$. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def inducedDistinctDegrees {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    58‚Üí    (S : Finset (Fin n)) : ‚Ñï :=
    59‚Üí  (S.image fun v => inducedDegree G S v).card
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/--
    62‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 637** (PROVED) [Er97d]:
    63‚Üí
    64‚ÜíFor every $C > 0$, there exist $c_1 > 0$, $c_2 > 0$, and $N_0$ such that for all
    65‚Üí$n \geq N_0$, if $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with clique number at most
    66‚Üí$C \cdot \log_2(n)$ and independence number at most $C \cdot \log_2(n)$, then there
    67‚Üíexists a subset $S$ of vertices with $|S| \geq c_1 \cdot n$ such that the induced
    68‚Üísubgraph $G[S]$ has at least $c_2 \cdot n^{1/2}$ distinct degrees.
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíProved by Bukh and Sudakov [BuSu07].
    71‚Üí-/
    72‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    73‚Üítheorem erdos_637 :
    74‚Üí    ‚àÄ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚Üí
    75‚Üí    ‚àÉ c‚ÇÅ : ‚Ñù, c‚ÇÅ > 0 ‚àß
    76‚Üí    ‚àÉ c‚ÇÇ : ‚Ñù, c‚ÇÇ > 0 ‚àß
    77‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    78‚Üí    ‚àÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    79‚Üí    (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), G.IsClique (‚ÜëS : Set (Fin n)) ‚Üí
    80‚Üí      (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * Real.logb 2 (‚Üën : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
    81‚Üí    (‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), G·∂ú.IsClique (‚ÜëS : Set (Fin n)) ‚Üí
    82‚Üí      (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * Real.logb 2 (‚Üën : ‚Ñù)) ‚Üí
    83‚Üí    ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• c‚ÇÅ * (‚Üën : ‚Ñù) ‚àß
    84‚Üí      (inducedDistinctDegrees G S : ‚Ñù) ‚â• c‚ÇÇ * (‚Üën : ‚Ñù) ^ ((1 : ‚Ñù) / 2) := by
    85‚Üí  sorry
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üíend Erdos637
    88‚Üí

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 621
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/621](https://www.erdosproblems.com/621)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës, Gallai, and Tuza on triangle-independent and triangle-transversal
    25‚Üíedge sets in graphs. Proved by Norin and Sun.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[EGT96] Erd≈ës, P., Gallai, T., and Tuza, Z., _Covering and independence in triangle
    28‚Üístructures_, Discrete Mathematics 150 (1996), 89-101.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[NoSu16] Norin, S. and Sun, Y., _Triangle-independent sets vs. triangle-transversals_,
    31‚ÜíEuropean Journal of Combinatorics 56 (2016), 95-104.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos621
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- Create an unordered pair from two vertices, representing an edge. -/
    39‚Üíabbrev mkEdge {V : Type*} (a b : V) : Sym2 V := Quot.mk _ (a, b)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíA set of edges $S$ is triangle-independent in $G$ if $S \subseteq E(G)$ and every
    43‚Üítriangle in $G$ contains at most one edge from $S$.
    44‚Üí-/
    45‚Üídef IsTriangleIndependent {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    46‚Üí    (S : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  S ‚äÜ G.edgeFinset ‚àß
    48‚Üí  ‚àÄ a b c : Fin n, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b c ‚Üí G.Adj a c ‚Üí
    49‚Üí    ¬¨(mkEdge a b ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge b c ‚àà S) ‚àß
    50‚Üí    ¬¨(mkEdge a b ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge a c ‚àà S) ‚àß
    51‚Üí    ¬¨(mkEdge b c ‚àà S ‚àß mkEdge a c ‚àà S)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíA set of edges $T$ is a triangle transversal in $G$ if $T \subseteq E(G)$ and every
    55‚Üítriangle in $G$ contains at least one edge from $T$.
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üídef IsTriangleTransversal {n : ‚Ñï} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    58‚Üí    (T : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    59‚Üí  T ‚äÜ G.edgeFinset ‚àß
    60‚Üí  ‚àÄ a b c : Fin n, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj b c ‚Üí G.Adj a c ‚Üí
    61‚Üí    mkEdge a b ‚àà T ‚à® mkEdge b c ‚àà T ‚à® mkEdge a c ‚àà T
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/--
    64‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 621** (Erd≈ës-Gallai-Tuza conjecture) [EGT96]:
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph on $n$ vertices. Define $\alpha_1(G)$ as the maximum number of edges
    67‚Üíforming a set that contains at most one edge from every triangle, and $\tau_1(G)$ as
    68‚Üíthe minimum number of edges forming a set that contains at least one edge from
    69‚Üíevery triangle.
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíConjecture: $\alpha_1(G) + \tau_1(G) \leq n^2/4$.
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíThis was proved by Norin and Sun [NoSu16].
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_621 (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    77‚Üí    (S : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)))
    78‚Üí    (hS : IsTriangleIndependent G S)
    79‚Üí    (hS_max : ‚àÄ S' : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)), IsTriangleIndependent G S' ‚Üí S'.card ‚â§ S.card)
    80‚Üí    (T : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)))
    81‚Üí    (hT : IsTriangleTransversal G T)
    82‚Üí    (hT_min : ‚àÄ T' : Finset (Sym2 (Fin n)), IsTriangleTransversal G T' ‚Üí T.card ‚â§ T'.card) :
    83‚Üí    4 * (S.card + T.card) ‚â§ n ^ 2 := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos621
    87‚Üí

    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 599
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/599](https://www.erdosproblems.com/599)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSometimes known as the Erd≈ës-Menger conjecture. Let $G$ be a (possibly infinite)
    25‚Üígraph and $A$, $B$ be disjoint independent sets of vertices. Must there exist a
    26‚Üífamily $P$ of disjoint paths between $A$ and $B$ and a set $S$ which contains
    27‚Üíexactly one vertex from each path in $P$, and such that every path between $A$
    28‚Üíand $B$ contains at least one vertex from $S$?
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíWhen $G$ is finite this is equivalent to Menger's theorem. Erd≈ës was interested
    31‚Üíin the case when $G$ is infinite. This was proved by Aharoni and Berger [AhBe09].
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Er81] Erd≈ës, P. (1981).
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[Er87] Erd≈ës, P. (1987).
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[AhBe09] Aharoni, R. and Berger, E., _Menger's theorem for infinite graphs_.
    38‚ÜíInventiones Mathematicae 176 (2009), 1-62.
    39‚Üí-/
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üínamespace Erdos599
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/--
    46‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 599 (Erd≈ës-Menger Conjecture) [Er81][Er87]:
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíFor any graph $G$ and disjoint independent sets $A$, $B$, there exists a family of
    49‚Üípairwise vertex-disjoint $A$-$B$ paths and a set $S$ containing exactly one vertex
    50‚Üífrom each path, such that $S$ separates $A$ from $B$ (every $A$-$B$ walk meets $S$).
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    53‚Üítheorem erdos_599 : answer(True) ‚Üî
    54‚Üí    ‚àÄ (V : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph V) (A B : Set V),
    55‚Üí      Disjoint A B ‚Üí

    29‚Üí/--
    30‚ÜíGiven a set `S` and an element `b` in an order `Œ≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite,
    31‚Üíwe define the partial density of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the proportion of elements in
    32‚Üí`{x ‚àà A | x < b}` that lie in `S ‚à© A`.
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThis definition was inspired from https://github.com/b-mehta/unit-fractions
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí@[inline]
    37‚Üínoncomputable abbrev partialDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    38‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) (b : Œ≤) : ‚Ñù :=
    39‚Üí  (Set.interIio (S ‚à© A) b).ncard / (Set.interIio A b).ncard

    41‚Üítheorem partialDensity_le_one {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    42‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) (b : Œ≤) : S.partialDensity A b ‚â§ 1 := by
    43‚Üí  apply div_le_one_of_le‚ÇÄ _ (Nat.cast_nonneg _)
    44‚Üí  exact mod_cast Set.ncard_le_ncard <| Set.inter_subset_inter_left _ inter_subset_right
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚ÜíGiven a set `S` in an order `Œ≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the upper
    48‚Üídensity of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the limsup of the partial densities of `S`
    49‚Üí(relative to `A`) for `b ‚Üí ‚àû`.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def upperDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    52‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : ‚Ñù :=
    53‚Üí  atTop.limsup fun (b : Œ≤) ‚Ü¶ S.partialDensity A b
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíGiven a set `S` in an order `Œ≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the lower
    57‚Üídensity of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the liminf of the partial densities of `S`
    58‚Üí(relative to `A`) for `b ‚Üí ‚àû`.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üínoncomputable def lowerDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    61‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : ‚Ñù :=

    63‚Üí

    65‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : S.lowerDensity A ‚â§ 1 := by
    66‚Üí  by_cases h : atTop (Œ± := Œ≤) = ‚ä•
    67‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [h, Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]
    68‚Üí  ¬∑ have : (atTop (Œ± := Œ≤)).NeBot := ‚ü®h‚ü©
    69‚Üí    apply Real.sSup_le (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) one_pos.le
    70‚Üí    simpa using hx.mono fun y hy ‚Ü¶ hy.trans (Set.partialDensity_le_one _ _ y)

    73‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : 0 ‚â§ S.lowerDensity A := by

    78‚Üí/--
    79‚ÜíA set `S` in an order `Œ≤` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    80‚Üídensity `Œ± : ‚Ñù` (relative to a set `A`) if the proportion of `x ‚àà S` such that `x < n`
    81‚Üíin `A` tends to `Œ±` as `n ‚Üí ‚àû`.

    83‚ÜíWhen `Œ≤ = ‚Ñï` this by default defines the natural density of a set
    84‚Üí(i.e., relative to all of `‚Ñï`).

    86‚Üídef HasDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    87‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (Œ± : ‚Ñù) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    88‚Üí  Tendsto (fun (b : Œ≤) => S.partialDensity A b) atTop (ùìù Œ±)
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí/--
    91‚ÜíA set `S` in an order `Œ≤` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    92‚Üípositive density (relative to a set `A`) if there exists a positive `Œ± : ‚Ñù` such that
    93‚Üí`S` has density `Œ±` (relative to a set `A`).
    94‚Üí-/
    95‚Üídef HasPosDensity {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤]
    96‚Üí    (S : Set Œ≤) (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    97‚Üí  ‚àÉ Œ± > 0, S.HasDensity Œ± A
    98‚Üí
    99‚Üínamespace HasDensity
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí-- TODO(mercuris): generalise these to non-univ `A`
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí/-- In a non-trivial partial order with a least element, the set of all
   104‚Üíelements has density one. -/
   105‚Üí@[simp]
   106‚Üítheorem univ {Œ≤ : Type*} [PartialOrder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrder Œ≤] [OrderBot Œ≤] [Nontrivial Œ≤] :
   107‚Üí    (@Set.univ Œ≤).HasDensity 1 := by
   108‚Üí  by_cases h : atTop (Œ± := Œ≤) = ‚ä•
   109‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [h, HasDensity]
   110‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [HasDensity, partialDensity]
   111‚Üí    let ‚ü®b, hb‚ü© := Set.Iio_eventually_ncard_ne_zero Œ≤
   112‚Üí    refine tendsto_const_nhds.congr' ?_
   113‚Üí    exact (eventually_ge_atTop b).mono fun n hn ‚Ü¶ (div_self <| mod_cast hb n hn).symm
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üítheorem univ_nat_hasDensity_one : (@Set.univ ‚Ñï).HasDensity 1 := univ
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí@[simp]
   118‚Üítheorem empty {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤] (A : Set Œ≤ := Set.univ) :
   119‚Üí    Set.HasDensity (‚àÖ : Set Œ≤) 0 A := by
   120‚Üí  simpa [HasDensity, partialDensity] using tendsto_const_nhds
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üítheorem mono {Œ≤ : Type*} [PartialOrder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrder Œ≤] [OrderBot Œ≤]
   123‚Üí    {S T : Set Œ≤} {Œ±S Œ±T : ‚Ñù} [(atTop (Œ± := Œ≤)).NeBot] (h : S ‚äÜ T) (hS : S.HasDensity Œ±S)
   124‚Üí    (hT : T.HasDensity Œ±T) : Œ±S ‚â§ Œ±T := by
   125‚Üí  rw [HasDensity] at hS hT
   126‚Üí  apply le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto hS hT
   127‚Üí  filter_upwards [eventually_ge_atTop ‚ä•] with b hb
   128‚Üí  apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_right
   129‚Üí  grw [Set.ncard_le_ncard (inter_subset_inter_left _ (inter_subset_inter_left _ h))]
   130‚Üí  exact Nat.cast_nonneg _
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üítheorem nonneg {Œ≤ : Type*} [Preorder Œ≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot Œ≤] [(atTop : Filter Œ≤).NeBot]
   133‚Üí    {S : Set Œ≤} {Œ± : ‚Ñù} (h : S.HasDensity Œ±) : 0 ‚â§ Œ± :=
   134‚Üí  le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto' empty h fun b => by simp [div_nonneg, partialDensity]
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üíend Set.HasDensity
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üínamespace Nat
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üíopen Set
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí/--
   143‚ÜíThe natural density of the set of even numbers is `1 / 2`.
   144‚Üí-/
   145‚Üítheorem hasDensity_even : {n : ‚Ñï | Even n}.HasDensity (1 / 2) := by
   146‚Üí  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   147‚Üí  have h {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : 1 ‚â§ n) : (({n : ‚Ñï | Even n} ‚à© Iio n).ncard : ‚Ñù) / n =
   148‚Üí      if Even n then 2‚Åª¬π else (n + 1 : ‚Ñù) /  n * 2‚Åª¬π := by
   149‚Üí    split_ifs with h
   150‚Üí    ¬∑ rw [‚Üê image_mul_two_Iio_even h, ncard_image_of_injective _
   151‚Üí          (mul_right_injective‚ÇÄ (by simp)), ncard_Iio,
   152‚Üí        cast_div_charZero (even_iff_two_dvd.mp h), cast_ofNat,
   153‚Üí        div_div_cancel_left' <| cast_ne_zero.2 (by linarith)]
   154‚Üí    ¬∑ replace h : Even (n + 1) := by simpa [Nat.even_add', ‚Üê Nat.not_even_iff_odd]
   155‚Üí      rw [‚Üê image_mul_two_Iio n, ncard_image_of_injective _
   156‚Üí          (mul_right_injective‚ÇÄ (by simp)), ncard_Iio,
   157‚Üí        cast_div (even_iff_two_dvd.mp h) (by norm_num), cast_add]; ring
   158‚Üí  refine Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ‚ü®1, fun n hn => (h hn).symm‚ü©)
   159‚Üí    (Tendsto.if' tendsto_const_nhds ?_)
   160‚Üí  replace h : Tendsto (fun (k : ‚Ñï) => 1 + 1 / (k : ‚Ñù)) atTop (ùìù 1) := by
   161‚Üí    simpa using Tendsto.const_add (M := ‚Ñù) _ tendsto_one_div_atTop_nhds_zero_nat
   162‚Üí  simpa using Tendsto.mul_const _ <|
   163‚Üí    Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ‚ü®1, fun k hk => by field_simp‚ü©) h
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí/-- A finite set has natural density zero. -/
   166‚Üítheorem hasDensity_zero_of_finite {S : Set ‚Ñï} (h : S.Finite) : S.HasDensity 0 := by
   167‚Üí  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   168‚Üí  have (n : ‚Ñï) : ((S ‚à© Set.Iio n).ncard : ‚Ñù) / n ‚â§ S.ncard / n := by
   169‚Üí    by_cases h‚ÇÄ : n = 0; simp [h‚ÇÄ]
   170‚Üí    exact div_le_div‚ÇÄ (by simp) (by simpa using Set.ncard_inter_le_ncard_left _ _ h)
   171‚Üí      (by simpa using n.pos_of_ne_zero h‚ÇÄ) le_rfl
   172‚Üí  exact tendsto_of_tendsto_of_tendsto_of_le_of_le tendsto_const_nhds
   173‚Üí    (tendsto_const_div_atTop_nhds_zero_nat _)
   174‚Üí    (fun _ => div_nonneg (cast_nonneg _) (cast_nonneg _)) this
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí/-- A set of positive natural density is infinite. -/
   177‚Üítheorem infinite_of_hasDensity_pos {S : Set ‚Ñï} {Œ± : ‚Ñù} (h : S.HasDensity Œ±) (hŒ± : Œ± ‚âÝ 0) :
   178‚Üí    S.Infinite :=
   179‚Üí  mt hasDensity_zero_of_finite fun h' => hŒ± (tendsto_nhds_unique h h')

   181‚Üíend Nat

   183‚Üí/-! ## Logarithmic Density -/
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üísection LogarithmicDensity
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üíopen Finset Real Classical
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíA set `A` of natural numbers has logarithmic density `d` if the sequence
   191‚Üí$(1 / \log n) \cdot \sum_{k \in A, k \le n} (1/k)$ converges to `d`.
   192‚ÜíLogarithmic density is a weaker notion than natural density: if a set has natural density `d`,
   193‚Üíthen it also has logarithmic density `d` (see `Set.HasDensity.hasLogDensity`), but the converse
   194‚Üíis false.
   195‚Üí-/
   196‚Üídef Set.HasLogDensity (A : Set ‚Ñï) (d : ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
   197‚Üí  Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï => (‚àë k ‚â§ n with k ‚àà A, (k : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π / .log n : ‚Ñù)) atTop (ùìù d)
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí/-- If a set has natural density `d`, then it also has logarithmic density `d`. -/
   200‚Üíproof_wanted Set.HasDensity.hasLogDensity {A : Set ‚Ñï} {d : ‚Ñù} (h : A.HasDensity d) : A.HasLogDensity d
   201‚Üí

    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30‚ÜíIt is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31‚Üínoncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    32‚Üí  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ‚àß IsTree T.coe }
    33‚Üí  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚Ñù)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := Fintype.card Œ±
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚Ñù)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset Œ±) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set Œ±))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset Œ±) : ‚Ñ§ := (s.card : ‚Ñ§) - (‚ãÉ v ‚àà (s : Set Œ±), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚Ñù)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚àß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ñï :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : Œ± => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚àß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚Ñù)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚Ñù)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    91‚Üí  (‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚Ñù¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚Ñù¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚àÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚àß (‚àÄ p ‚àà P, p.IsPath) ‚àß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚àû` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph
   129‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 524
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/524](https://www.erdosproblems.com/524)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of Salem and Zygmund. For any $t \in (0,1)$ let
    25‚Üí$t = \sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \varepsilon_k(t) 2^{-k}$ be the binary expansion (where
    26‚Üí$\varepsilon_k(t) \in \{0,1\}$). What is the correct order of magnitude (for almost all
    27‚Üí$t \in (0,1)$) of
    28‚Üí$$M_n(t) = \max_{x \in [-1,1]} \left|\sum_{k \le n} (-1)^{\varepsilon_k(t)} x^k\right|?$$
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThe binary digits $\varepsilon_k(t)$ are i.i.d. Bernoulli$(1/2)$ under Lebesgue measure, so
    31‚Üí$(-1)^{\varepsilon_k(t)}$ are i.i.d. Rademacher random variables.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí**Known partial results:**
    34‚Üí- Erd≈ës (unpublished) showed: for a.a. $t$ and every $\delta > 0$,
    35‚Üí  $\lim_{n \to \infty} M_n(t)/n^{1/2 - \delta} = \infty$.
    36‚Üí- Chung showed: for a.a. $t$, there exist infinitely many $n$ such that
    37‚Üí  $M_n(t) \ll (n / \log \log n)^{1/2}$.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Finset BigOperators Set Asymptotics
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üínamespace Erdos524
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- A random variable is Rademacher distributed: it takes values in $\{-1, 1\}$
    45‚Üíwith equal probability. -/
    46‚Üídef IsRademacher {Œ© : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Œ©] (Œº : Measure Œ©) (X : Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  (‚àÄ œâ, X œâ = 1 ‚à® X œâ = -1) ‚àß
    48‚Üí  Œº {œâ | X œâ = 1} = Œº {œâ | X œâ = -1}
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The supremum of $\left|\sum_{k=1}^n \varepsilon_k x^k\right|$ over $x \in [-1, 1]$. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def supNormInterval (Œµ : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù) (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñù :=
    52‚Üí  sSup {y : ‚Ñù | ‚àÉ x : ‚Ñù, x ‚àà Icc (-1 : ‚Ñù) 1 ‚àß
    53‚Üí    y = |‚àë k ‚àà Finset.range n, Œµ (k + 1) * x ^ (k + 1)|}
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- What is the correct order of magnitude of $M_n(t)$ for almost all $t$?
    56‚ÜíThe answer $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{R}$ should satisfy
    57‚Üí$M_n(\omega) =\Theta(f(n))$ as $n \to \infty$ for a.a. $\omega$. -/
    58‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 42 60]
    59‚Üítheorem erdos_524
    60‚Üí    {Œ© : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Œ©] {Œº : Measure Œ©} [IsProbabilityMeasure Œº]
    61‚Üí    {Œµ : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù}
    62‚Üí    (hRad : ‚àÄ k, IsRademacher Œº (Œµ k))
    63‚Üí    (hIndep : iIndepFun Œµ Œº) :
    64‚Üí    ‚àÄ·µê œâ ‚àÇŒº,
    65‚Üí      (fun n => supNormInterval (fun k => Œµ k œâ) n) =Œò[atTop]
    66‚Üí        (answer(sorry) : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù) := by

    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/-- Erd≈ës showed that for almost all $\omega$ and every $\delta > 0$,

    74‚Üí    {Œµ : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù}
    75‚Üí    (hRad : ‚àÄ k, IsRademacher Œº (Œµ k))

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 526
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíDvoretzky's covering problem: given a sequence of arc lengths $a_n \to 0$ with $\sum a_n = \infty$,
    23‚Üíarcs of these lengths placed independently and uniformly at random on the unit circle cover the
    24‚Üíentire circle with probability one if and only if $\sum \exp(a_1+\cdots+a_n)/n^2 = \infty$.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/526](https://www.erdosproblems.com/526)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[Er61] Erd≈ës, P., _Some unsolved problems_. Magyar Tud. Akad. Mat. Kutat√≥ Int. K√∂zl. 6 (1961),
    29‚Üíp. 253.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Sh72] Shepp, L. A., _Covering the circle with random arcs_. Israel J. Math. 11 (1972), 328‚Äì345.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter Finset BigOperators Set
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos526
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- Point $y$ is covered by an arc of length $\ell$ starting at $x$ on the unit circle
    39‚Üí$\mathbb{R}/\mathbb{Z}$. The clockwise arc-distance from $x$ to $y$ is the fractional
    40‚Üípart of $(y - x)$. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def CircArcCovers (x y ‚Ñì : ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  Int.fract (y - x) < ‚Ñì
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The full unit circle $[0,1)$ is covered by arcs at centers $\omega(n)$ of lengths $a(n)$. -/
    45‚Üínoncomputable def CircleFullyCovered (œâ a : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù) : Prop :=
    46‚Üí  ‚àÄ y : ‚Ñù, 0 ‚â§ y ‚Üí y < 1 ‚Üí ‚àÉ n : ‚Ñï, CircArcCovers (œâ n) y (a n)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/--
    49‚ÜíDvoretzky's covering problem [Er61, p. 253] (solved by Shepp [Sh72]).
    50‚Üí
    51‚ÜíLet $a_n \geq 0$ with $a_n \to 0$ and $\sum a_n = \infty$. If we independently and uniformly
    52‚Üíplace random arcs of length $a_n$ on the unit circle, Shepp showed the circle is covered with
    53‚Üíprobability $1$ if and only if
    54‚Üí$$
    55‚Üí  \sum_n \frac{\exp(a_1 + \cdots + a_n)}{n^2} = \infty.
    56‚Üí$$
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíThe probability space $(\Omega, \mu)$ carries independent $\mathrm{Uniform}[0,1)$ random
    59‚Üívariables $X(n)$ giving the starting point of the $n$-th arc.
    60‚Üí-/
    61‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 60]
    62‚Üítheorem erdos_526
    63‚Üí    {Œ© : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Œ©] {Œº : Measure Œ©} [IsProbabilityMeasure Œº]
    64‚Üí    (a : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
    65‚Üí    (ha_nonneg : ‚àÄ n, 0 ‚â§ a n)
    66‚Üí    (ha_tendsto : Tendsto a atTop (nhds 0))
    67‚Üí    (ha_diverges : ¬¨Summable a)
    68‚Üí    {X : ‚Ñï ‚Üí Œ© ‚Üí ‚Ñù}
    69‚Üí    (hX_meas : ‚àÄ n, Measurable (X n))
    70‚Üí    (hX_unif : ‚àÄ n, Measure.map (X n) Œº = volume.restrict (Ico (0 : ‚Ñù) 1))
    71‚Üí    (hX_indep : iIndepFun X Œº) :
    72‚Üí    (‚àÄ·µê œâ ‚àÇŒº, CircleFullyCovered (fun n => X n œâ) a) ‚Üî
    73‚Üí      ¬¨Summable (fun n => Real.exp (‚àë i ‚àà range (n + 1), a i) / ((n : ‚Ñù) + 1) ^ 2) := by
    74‚Üí  sorry
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üíend Erdos526

    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/515](https://www.erdosproblems.com/515)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(z)$ be an entire function, not a polynomial. Does there exist a locally
    25‚Üírectifiable path $C$ tending to infinity such that, for every $\lambda > 0$, the integral
    26‚Üí$\int_C |f(z)|^{-\lambda} \, |dz|$ is finite?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíThis was proved in the affirmative. The case when $f$ has finite order was proved
    29‚Üíby Zhang [Zh77]. The general case was proved by Lewis, Rossi, and Weitsman
    30‚Üí[LRW84], who in fact proved this with $|f|$ replaced by $e^u$ where $u$ is any
    31‚Üísubharmonic function.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Zh77] Zhang, G., _Asymptotic paths of entire functions_, 1977.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[LRW84] Lewis, J., Rossi, J. and Weitsman, A., _On the growth of subharmonic
    36‚Üífunctions along paths_, Ark. Mat. 22 (1984), 109-114.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen MeasureTheory Filter Set
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos515
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 515: For any entire function $f$ that is not a polynomial, there
    45‚Üíexists a locally rectifiable path $\gamma$ tending to infinity such that for every
    46‚Üí$\lambda > 0$, the line integral $\int_\gamma |f(z)|^{-\lambda} \, |dz|$ is finite.
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíThe path $\gamma : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{C}$ is parametrized on $[0, \infty)$, is continuous
    49‚Üíwith locally bounded variation (i.e., locally rectifiable), and satisfies
    50‚Üí$\|\gamma(t)\| \to \infty$ as $t \to \infty$. The integral
    51‚Üí$\int_\gamma |f(z)|^{-\lambda} \, |dz|$ is expressed as
    52‚Üí$\int_0^\infty \|f(\gamma(t))\|^{-\lambda} \cdot \|\gamma'(t)\| \, dt$.
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíThe case when $f$ has finite order was proved by Zhang [Zh77]. The general case
    55‚Üíwas proved by Lewis, Rossi, and Weitsman [LRW84].
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 30]
    58‚Üítheorem erdos_515 : answer(True) ‚Üî
    59‚Üí    ‚àÄ (f : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ),
    60‚Üí      Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f ‚Üí
    61‚Üí      (¬¨ ‚àÉ p : Polynomial ‚ÑÇ, ‚àÄ z, f z = p.eval z) ‚Üí
    62‚Üí      ‚àÉ (Œ≥ : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ),
    63‚Üí        Continuous Œ≥ ‚àß
    64‚Üí        LocallyBoundedVariationOn Œ≥ (Ici 0) ‚àß
    65‚Üí        Tendsto (fun t => ‚ÄñŒ≥ t‚Äñ) atTop atTop ‚àß
    66‚Üí        ‚àÄ l : ‚Ñù, 0 < l ‚Üí
    67‚Üí          IntegrableOn
    68‚Üí            (fun t => ‚Äñf (Œ≥ t)‚Äñ ^ (-l) * ‚Äñderiv Œ≥ t‚Äñ)
    69‚Üí            (Ici 0)
    70‚Üí            volume := by
    71‚Üí  sorry
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üíend Erdos515
    74‚Üí

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Clique

    21‚Üí
    22‚Üívariable {V Œ± Œπ : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {n : ‚Ñï}
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "œá(" G ")" => chromaticNumber G
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable : n ‚â§ G.chromaticNumber ‚Üî ‚àÄ m, G.Colorable m ‚Üí n ‚â§ m := by
    29‚Üí  simp [chromaticNumber]
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_coloring :
    32‚Üí    n ‚â§ G.chromaticNumber ‚Üî ‚àÄ m, G.Coloring (Fin m) ‚Üí n ‚â§ m := by
    33‚Üí  simp [le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable, Colorable]
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üílemma lt_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable : n < G.chromaticNumber ‚Üî ¬¨ G.Colorable n := by
    36‚Üí  rw [‚Üê chromaticNumber_le_iff_colorable, not_le]
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable (hn : n ‚âÝ 0) :
    39‚Üí    n ‚â§ G.chromaticNumber ‚Üî ¬¨ G.Colorable (n - 1) := by
    40‚Üí  let n + 1 := n; simp [ENat.add_one_le_iff, lt_chromaticNumber_iff_not_colorable]
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üílemma Coloring.injective_comp_of_pairwise_adj (C : G.Coloring Œ±) (f : Œπ ‚Üí V)
    43‚Üí    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : (C ‚àò f).Injective :=
    44‚Üí  Function.injective_iff_pairwise_ne.2 fun _i _j hij ‚Ü¶ C.valid <| hf hij
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üílemma Colorable.card_le_of_pairwise_adj (hG : G.Colorable n) (f : Œπ ‚Üí V)
    47‚Üí    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : Nat.card Œπ ‚â§ n := by
    48‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®C‚ü© := hG
    49‚Üí  simpa using Nat.card_le_card_of_injective _ (C.injective_comp_of_pairwise_adj f hf)
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üílemma le_chromaticNumber_of_pairwise_adj (hn : n ‚â§ Nat.card Œπ) (f : Œπ ‚Üí V)
    52‚Üí    (hf : Pairwise fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j)) : n ‚â§ G.chromaticNumber :=
    53‚Üí  le_chromaticNumber_iff_colorable.2 fun _m hm ‚Ü¶ hn.trans <| hm.card_le_of_pairwise_adj f hf
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üílemma card_div_indepNum_le_chromaticNumber : ‚åà(Nat.card V / Œ±(G) : ‚Ñö‚â•0)‚åâ‚Çä ‚â§ G.chromaticNumber := by
    56‚Üí  cases finite_or_infinite V
    57‚Üí  swap; ¬∑ simp
    58‚Üí  cases nonempty_fintype V
    59‚Üí  simp only [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, le_chromaticNumber_iff_coloring, Nat.ceil_le]
    60‚Üí  refine fun m c ‚Ü¶ div_le_of_le_mul‚ÇÄ (by simp) (by simp) ?_
    61‚Üí  norm_cast
    62‚Üí  rw [‚Üê mul_one (Fintype.card V), ‚Üê Fintype.card_fin m]
    63‚Üí  refine Finset.card_mul_le_card_mul (c ¬∑ = ¬∑)
    64‚Üí    (by simp [Finset.bipartiteAbove, Finset.filter_nonempty_iff])
    65‚Üí    fun b _ ‚Ü¶ IsIndepSet.card_le_indepNum ?_
    66‚Üí  simpa [IsIndepSet, Set.Pairwise] using fun x hx y hy _ ‚Ü¶ c.not_adj_of_mem_colorClass hx hy
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíinstance (f : Œπ ‚Üí V) : Std.Symm fun i j ‚Ü¶ G.Adj (f i) (f j) where symm _ _ := .symm
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üívariable (G) in
    71‚Üí/-- A set of edges is critical if deleting them reduces the chromatic number. -/
    72‚Üídef IsCriticalEdges (edges : Set (Sym2 V)) : Prop :=
    73‚Üí  (G.deleteEdges edges).chromaticNumber < G.chromaticNumber
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üívariable (G) in
    76‚Üí/-- An edge is critical if deleting it reduces the chromatic number. -/
    77‚Üídef IsCriticalEdge (e : Sym2 V) : Prop := G.IsCriticalEdges ({e} : Set (Sym2 V))
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/--
    80‚ÜíA set of vertices is critical if deleting them reduces the chromatic number.
    81‚Üí-/
    82‚Üídef Subgraph.IsCriticalVerts (verts : Set V) (G' : G.Subgraph) : Prop :=
    83‚Üí  (G'.deleteVerts verts).coe.chromaticNumber < G'.coe.chromaticNumber
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/--
    86‚ÜíA vertex is critical if deleting it reduces the chromatic number.
    87‚Üí-/
    88‚Üídef Subgraph.IsCriticalVertex (v : V) (G' : G.Subgraph) : Prop := G'.IsCriticalVerts {v}
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üívariable (G)
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/--
    93‚ÜíA graph `G` is `k`-critical (or vertex-critical) if its chromatic number is `k`,
    94‚Üíand deleting any single vertex reduces the chromatic number.
    95‚Üí-/
    96‚Üídef IsCritical (k : ‚Ñï) : Prop := G.chromaticNumber = k ‚àß ‚àÄ v, (‚ä§ : G.Subgraph).IsCriticalVertex v
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üítheorem not_isCritical_of_fintype_lt [Fintype V] (k : ‚Ñï) (hk : Fintype.card V < k) :
    99‚Üí   ¬¨G.IsCritical k := by
   100‚Üí  simp [IsCritical]
   101‚Üí  intro h
   102‚Üí  have := h ‚ñ∏ SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber_le_iff_colorable.2 G.colorable_of_fintype
   103‚Üí  simp at this
   104‚Üí  grind
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üítheorem colorable_iff_induce_eq_bot (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ñï) :
   109‚Üí    G.Colorable n ‚Üî ‚àÉ coloring : V ‚Üí Fin n, ‚àÄ i, G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ‚ä• := by
   110‚Üí  refine ‚ü®fun ‚ü®a, h‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®a, by aesop‚ü©, fun ‚ü®w, h‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®w, @fun a b h_adj ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©‚ü©
   111‚Üí  specialize h (w a)
   112‚Üí  contrapose h
   113‚Üí  intro hG
   114‚Üí  have : ¬¨ ((SimpleGraph.induce {v | w v = w a} G).Adj ‚ü®a, by rfl‚ü© ‚ü®b, by simp_all‚ü©) :=
   115‚Üí    hG ‚ñ∏ fun a ‚Ü¶ a
   116‚Üí  exact this h_adj
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üídef Cocolorable (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ñï) : Prop := ‚àÉ coloring : V ‚Üí Fin n,
   119‚Üí  ‚àÄ i, G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ‚ä• ‚à® G.induce {v | coloring v = i} = ‚ä§
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üíexample (G : SimpleGraph V) (n : ‚Ñï) : G.Colorable n ‚Üí SimpleGraph.Cocolorable G n := by
   122‚Üí  simp [colorable_iff_induce_eq_bot, Cocolorable]
   123‚Üí  aesop
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí/--
   126‚ÜíThe chromatic number of a graph is the minimal number of colors needed to color it.
   127‚ÜíThis is `‚ä§` (infinity) iff `G` isn't colorable with finitely many colors.
   128‚Üí
   129‚ÜíIf `G` is colorable, then `ENat.toNat G.chromaticNumber` is the `‚Ñï`-valued chromatic number.
   130‚Üí-/
   131‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ö n ‚àà setOf G.Cocolorable, (n : ‚Ñï‚àû)
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí/-- The chromatic cardinal is the minimal number of colors need to color it. In contrast to
   134‚Üí`chromaticNumber`, which assigns `‚ä§ : ‚Ñï‚àû` to all non-finitely colorable graphs, this definition
   135‚Üíreturns a `Cardinal` and can therefore distinguish between different infinite chromatic numbers. -/
   136‚Üínoncomputable def chromaticCardinal.{u} {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Cardinal :=
   137‚Üí  sInf {Œ∫ : Cardinal | ‚àÉ (C : Type u) (_ : Cardinal.mk C = Œ∫), Nonempty (G.Coloring C)}
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- The maximum size of the union of k finite independent sets. -/
   140‚Üínoncomputable def indepNumK (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
   141‚Üí  sSup {n | ‚àÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí Set V, (‚àÄ i, G.IsIndepSet (f i)) ‚àß (‚ãÉ i, f i).ncard = n}
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí/-- A finite graph is CDS-colorable if it has a proper coloring
   144‚Üíby natural numbers such that for all `k > 0`, the number of
   145‚Üívertices with color `< k` equals the maximum size of
   146‚Üíthe union of `k` independent sets. -/
   147‚Üídef CDSColorable [Fintype Œ±] {G : SimpleGraph Œ±} : Prop :=
   148‚Üí    ‚àÉ (C : G.Coloring Nat), ‚àÄ k : Nat,
   149‚Üí   ‚àë i < k, (C.colorClass i).ncard = indepNumK G k
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí/-- A homomorphism is rainbow if it maps distinct edges to distinct colors. -/
   152‚Üídef IsRainbow {Œ± V : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph Œ±} {G : SimpleGraph V} (f : H ‚Üíg G) {C : Type*}
   153‚Üí    (c : Sym2 V ‚Üí C) : Prop :=
   154‚Üí  Function.Injective fun e : H.edgeSet => c (Sym2.map f e)
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí/--
   157‚ÜíThe anti-Ramsey number $\mathrm{AR}(n, H)$: maximum colors to edge-color $K_n$ without rainbow $H$.
   158‚Üí-/
   159‚Üínoncomputable def antiRamseyNum {Œ± : Type*} [Fintype Œ±] (H : SimpleGraph Œ±) (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
   160‚Üí  sSup {k | ‚àÉ c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin k, ‚àÄ f : H ‚Üíg ‚ä§, ¬¨IsRainbow f c}
   161‚Üí

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 1024
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1024](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1024)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(n)$ be such that every $3$-uniform linear hypergraph on $n$ vertices contains
    25‚Üían independent set on $f(n)$ vertices.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíA hypergraph is linear if $|A \cap B| \leq 1$ for all edges $A$ and $B$. An independent
    28‚Üíset of vertices is one which contains no edges. A $3$-uniform linear hypergraph
    29‚Üíis sometimes called a partial Steiner triple system.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíErd≈ës proved $n^{1/2} \ll f(n) \ll n^{2/3}$. Phelps and R√∂dl [PhRo86] proved
    32‚Üí$f(n) \asymp (n \log n)^{1/2}$.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[PhRo86] Phelps, K. T. and R√∂dl, V., _Steiner triple systems with minimum independence
    35‚Üínumber_. Ars Combin. 21 (1986), 167‚Äì172.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen Finset
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos1024
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph on `Fin n`: a family of $3$-element subsets. -/
    43‚Üístructure Hypergraph3Uniform (n : ‚Ñï) where
    44‚Üí  edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))
    45‚Üí  uniform : ‚àÄ e ‚àà edges, e.card = 3
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A $3$-uniform hypergraph is *linear* if any two distinct edges share
    48‚Üí    at most one vertex. -/
    49‚Üídef Hypergraph3Uniform.IsLinear {n : ‚Ñï} (H : Hypergraph3Uniform n) : Prop :=
    50‚Üí  ‚àÄ e‚ÇÅ ‚àà H.edges, ‚àÄ e‚ÇÇ ‚àà H.edges, e‚ÇÅ ‚âÝ e‚ÇÇ ‚Üí (e‚ÇÅ ‚à© e‚ÇÇ).card ‚â§ 1
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/-- A set $S$ of vertices is *independent* in $H$ if no edge of $H$ is
    53‚Üí    contained in $S$. -/
    54‚Üídef Hypergraph3Uniform.IsIndependent {n : ‚Ñï} (H : Hypergraph3Uniform n)
    55‚Üí    (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    56‚Üí  ‚àÄ e ‚àà H.edges, ¬¨(e ‚äÜ S)
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- The independence number of $H$: the maximum size of an independent set. -/
    59‚Üínoncomputable def Hypergraph3Uniform.independenceNumber {n : ‚Ñï}
    60‚Üí    (H : Hypergraph3Uniform n) : ‚Ñï :=
    61‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), H.IsIndependent S ‚àß S.card = k}
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- `linearHypergraphIndNum n` is the minimum independence number over all
    64‚Üí    $3$-uniform linear hypergraphs on $n$ vertices, i.e. the largest $f$ such
    65‚Üí    that every such hypergraph has an independent set of size at least $f$. -/
    66‚Üínoncomputable def linearHypergraphIndNum (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    67‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÉ H : Hypergraph3Uniform n, H.IsLinear ‚àß
    68‚Üí    H.independenceNumber = k}
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/--
    71‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 1024 (solved by Phelps‚ÄìR√∂dl [PhRo86]):
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí$f(n) \asymp (n \log n)^{1/2}$, where $f(n)$ is the minimum independence number over
    74‚Üíall $3$-uniform linear hypergraphs on $n$ vertices.
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíThere exist constants $C_1, C_2 > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    77‚Üí$$C_1 \sqrt{n \log n} \leq f(n) \leq C_2 \sqrt{n \log n}.$$
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_1024 :
    81‚Üí    ‚àÉ C‚ÇÅ C‚ÇÇ : ‚Ñù, 0 < C‚ÇÅ ‚àß 0 < C‚ÇÇ ‚àß
    82‚Üí    ‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• N‚ÇÄ ‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 1025
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1025](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1025)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f$ be a function from all pairs of elements in $\{1,\ldots,n\}$ to $\{1,\ldots,n\}$ such
    25‚Üíthat $f(x,y) \neq x$ and $f(x,y) \neq y$ for all $x,y$. We call $X \subseteq \{1,\ldots,n\}$
    26‚Üíindependent if whenever $x,y \in X$ we have $f(x,y) \notin X$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíLet $g(n)$ be such that, in every function $f$, there is an independent set of
    29‚Üísize at least $g(n)$. Estimate $g(n)$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíA question of Erd≈ës and Hajnal [ErHa58], who proved
    32‚Üí$n^{1/3} \ll g(n) \ll (n \log n)^{1/2}$.
    33‚ÜíSpencer [Sp72] proved $g(n) \gg n^{1/2}$.
    34‚ÜíConlon, Fox, and Sudakov [CFS16] proved $g(n) \ll n^{1/2}$.
    35‚ÜíThus $g(n) = \Theta(\sqrt{n})$.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[ErHa58] Erd≈ës, P. and Hajnal, A., 1958.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[Sp72] Spencer, J., 1972.
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[CFS16] Conlon, D., Fox, J., and Sudakov, B., 2016.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üíopen Finset
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üínamespace Erdos1025
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- A pair function on `Fin n` assigns to each pair of distinct elements
    49‚Üía third element different from both. -/
    50‚Üídef IsValidPairFunction (n : ‚Ñï) (f : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Fin n) : Prop :=
    51‚Üí  ‚àÄ x y : Fin n, x ‚âÝ y ‚Üí f x y ‚âÝ x ‚àß f x y ‚âÝ y
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- A set $S \subseteq \operatorname{Fin} n$ is independent with respect to $f$ if for all
    54‚Üídistinct $x, y \in S$, we have $f(x,y) \notin S$. -/
    55‚Üídef IsIndependentPairSet (n : ‚Ñï) (f : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Fin n)
    56‚Üí    (S : Finset (Fin n)) : Prop :=
    57‚Üí  ‚àÄ x ‚àà S, ‚àÄ y ‚àà S, x ‚âÝ y ‚Üí f x y ‚àâ S
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/--
    60‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 1025, lower bound (Spencer [Sp72]):
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíFor every valid pair function $f$ on $\operatorname{Fin} n$, there exists an independent set
    63‚Üíof size at least $C \cdot \sqrt{n}$, for some absolute constant $C > 0$.
    64‚Üí-/
    65‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    66‚Üítheorem erdos_1025 :
    67‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ f : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Fin n,
    68‚Üí    IsValidPairFunction n f ‚Üí
    69‚Üí    ‚àÉ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentPairSet n f S ‚àß
    70‚Üí      (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• C * Real.sqrt n := by
    71‚Üí  sorry
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí/--
    74‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 1025, upper bound (Conlon‚ÄìFox‚ÄìSudakov [CFS16]):
    75‚Üí
    76‚ÜíThere exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $n$,
    77‚Üíthere exists a valid pair function $f$ on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ for which every independent
    78‚Üíset has size at most $C \cdot \sqrt{n}$.
    79‚Üí-/
    80‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    81‚Üítheorem erdos_1025.variants.upper_bound :
    82‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, C > 0 ‚àß ‚àÉ n‚ÇÄ : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, n ‚â• n‚ÇÄ ‚Üí
    83‚Üí    ‚àÉ f : Fin n ‚Üí Fin n ‚Üí Fin n, IsValidPairFunction n f ‚àß
    84‚Üí    ‚àÄ S : Finset (Fin n), IsIndependentPairSet n f S ‚Üí
    85‚Üí      (S.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ C * Real.sqrt n := by
    86‚Üí  sorry
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üíend Erdos1025

    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 22
    21‚Üí

    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $\varepsilon > 0$ and $n$ be sufficiently large depending on $\varepsilon$. Is there a graph on
    25‚Üí$n$ vertices with $\geq n^2/8$ many edges which contains no $K_4$ such that the largest
    26‚Üíindependent set has size at most $\varepsilon n$?

    29‚Üí$\mathrm{rt}(n; k, \ell)$ is the Ramsey‚ÄìTur√°n number.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíConjectured by Bollob√°s and Erd≈ës [BoEr76], who proved the existence of such a graph with
    32‚Üí$(1/8 + o(1))n^2$ many edges. Proved by Fox, Loh, and Zhao [FLZ15], who showed that for every
    33‚Üí$n \geq 1$ there exists a $K_4$-free graph on $n$ vertices with $\geq n^2/8$ edges and
    34‚Üíindependence number $\ll (\log \log n)^{3/2} / (\log n)^{1/2} \cdot n$.
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[BoEr76] Bollob√°s, B. and Erd≈ës, P., _On a Ramsey‚ÄìTur√°n type problem_, J. Combin. Theory Ser. B, 1976.

    38‚Üí[FLZ15] Fox, J., Loh, P.-S., and Zhao, Y., _The critical window for the classical Ramsey‚ÄìTur√°n problem_, Combinatorica, 2015.

    46‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®S, hS, -‚ü© := this
    47‚Üí  calc n = S.card := hS.symm
    48‚Üí    _ ‚â§ Fintype.card (Fin m) := Finset.card_le_univ S
    49‚Üí    _ = m := Fintype.card_fin m
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- `hypergraphRamsey r r = r` for any `r`. -/
    52‚Üítheorem hypergraphRamsey_self (r : ‚Ñï) : hypergraphRamsey r r = r := by
    53‚Üí  have h_mem : r ‚àà { m | ‚àÄ (c : Finset (Fin m) ‚Üí Bool),
    54‚Üí      ‚àÉ (S : Finset (Fin m)), S.card = m ‚àß
    55‚Üí        ‚àÉ (color : Bool), ‚àÄ (e : Finset (Fin m)), e ‚äÜ S ‚Üí e.card = r ‚Üí c e = color } := by
    56‚Üí    intro c
    57‚Üí    refine ‚ü®Finset.univ, ?_, c Finset.univ, ?_‚ü©
    58‚Üí    ¬∑ simp [Fintype.card_fin]
    59‚Üí    ¬∑ intro e _ he_card
    60‚Üí      congr 1
    61‚Üí      exact Finset.eq_univ_of_card e (he_card.trans (Fintype.card_fin r).symm)
    62‚Üí  exact le_antisymm (Nat.sInf_le h_mem) (le_hypergraphRamsey r r ‚ü®r, h_mem‚ü©)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üíend Combinatorics

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 112
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/112](https://www.erdosproblems.com/112)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of Erd≈ës and Rado on directed Ramsey numbers $k(n,m)$: the minimal $k$ such that
    25‚Üíany directed graph on $k$ vertices must contain either an independent set of size $n$ or a
    26‚Üítransitive tournament of size $m$. Determine $k(n,m)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[ErRa67] Erd≈ës, P. and Rado, R., _Partition relations and transitivity domains of binary
    29‚Üírelations_, J. London Math. Soc. (1967).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[LaMi97] Larson, J. and Mitchell, W., _On a problem of Erd≈ës and Rado_, Ann. Comb. (1997).
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos112
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- A directed graph on vertex type $V$: an irreflexive binary relation representing
    37‚Üídirected edges ($\mathrm{adj}(u, v)$ means there is a directed edge from $u$ to $v$). -/
    38‚Üístructure Digraph (V : Type*) where
    39‚Üí  adj : V ‚Üí V ‚Üí Prop
    40‚Üí  loopless : ‚àÄ v, ¬¨ adj v v
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- An independent set in a directed graph: a set $S$ of vertices with no directed
    43‚Üíedges between any two of its members (in either direction). -/
    44‚Üídef Digraph.IsIndepSet {V : Type*} (G : Digraph V) (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  ‚àÄ u ‚àà S, ‚àÄ v ‚àà S, ¬¨ G.adj u v
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- A transitive tournament on vertex set $S$ in directed graph $G$: there is a bijection
    48‚Üí$f : \mathrm{Fin}(|S|) \to S$ such that $G.\mathrm{adj}(f(i), f(j))$ holds
    49‚Üíif and only if $i < j$. This encodes a total ordering of $S$ compatible with the
    50‚Üíedge relation. -/
    51‚Üídef Digraph.IsTransTournament {V : Type*} (G : Digraph V) (S : Finset V) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚àÉ f : Fin S.card ‚Üí {x : V // x ‚àà S}, Function.Bijective f ‚àß
    53‚Üí    ‚àÄ i j : Fin S.card, G.adj (f i : V) (f j : V) ‚Üî i < j
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- The directed Ramsey number $k(n,m)$: the minimal $k$ such that every directed graph
    56‚Üíon $k$ vertices contains either an independent set of size $n$ or a transitive
    57‚Üítournament of size $m$. -/
    58‚Üínoncomputable def dirRamseyNum (n m : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
    59‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ñï | ‚àÄ (V : Type) [Fintype V], Fintype.card V = k ‚Üí
    60‚Üí    ‚àÄ G : Digraph V,
    61‚Üí      (‚àÉ S : Finset V, S.card = n ‚àß G.IsIndepSet S) ‚à®
    62‚Üí      (‚àÉ S : Finset V, S.card = m ‚àß G.IsTransTournament S)}
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíErd≈ës Problem 112: Determine the directed Ramsey number $k(n,m)$.
    66‚ÜíThe exact value is still open.
    67‚Üí-/
    68‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    69‚Üítheorem erdos_112 :
    70‚Üí    ‚àÄ n m : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ n ‚Üí 2 ‚â§ m ‚Üí
    71‚Üí      dirRamseyNum n m = answer(sorry) := by
    72‚Üí  sorry
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí/--
    75‚ÜíErd≈ës‚ÄìRado upper bound [ErRa67]:
    76‚Üí$$k(n,m) \leq \frac{2^{m-1} (n-1)^m + n - 2}{2n - 3}.$$
    77‚Üí-/
    78‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    79‚Üítheorem erdos_112.variants.erdos_rado_upper_bound :
    80‚Üí    ‚àÄ n m : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ n ‚Üí 2 ‚â§ m ‚Üí
    81‚Üí      dirRamseyNum n m ‚â§ (2 ^ (m - 1) * (n - 1) ^ m + n - 2) / (2 * n - 3) := by
    82‚Üí  sorry
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí/--
    85‚ÜíLarson‚ÄìMitchell bound [LaMi97]: $k(n, 3) \leq n^2$.
    86‚Üí-/
    87‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    88‚Üítheorem erdos_112.variants.larson_mitchell :
    89‚Üí    ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, 2 ‚â§ n ‚Üí
    90‚Üí      dirRamseyNum n 3 ‚â§ n ^ 2 := by
    91‚Üí  sorry
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üíend Erdos112
    94‚Üí
